refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) for operational use, ensuring data accuracy and availability for all stakeholders.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly address timeliness, data accuracy, and availability, which are critical for operational effectiveness and safety in a high-risk environment.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of worksite model updates under representative operational scenarios; verify data accuracy through cross-validation with sensor inputs; confirm availability via system uptime and interface responsiveness tests.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within 0.5 s latency.
FR2: Update the centralized worksite model with processed data ensuring consistency and accuracy within 0.3 s latency.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model data to all relevant modules and stakeholders within 0.2 s latency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Real-time telemetry ingestion and preprocessing pipeline.
DP2: Centralized worksite model database with in-memory caching for low-latency updates.
DP3: Event-driven publish-subscribe distribution mechanism for worksite updates.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of DP1, DP2, and DP3 to meet the latency and accuracy requirements.

potential_sources: Data synchronization between telemetry ingestion and model update.
Latency dependencies between model update and distribution.
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically decoupled with well-defined interfaces; event-driven architecture reduces tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link latency is stable and within acceptable bounds.
Risk of data loss or delay due to network congestion or hardware failure.
Assumes sufficient processing power in NGHE solution to handle real-time data.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor network performance and consider fallback mechanisms for telemetry data to mitigate risks.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Contains telemetry ingestion, centralized worksite model, and distribution services as a cohesive software platform.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time telemetry data from Vehicle modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model data to Teleoperation and Vehicle modules for task execution.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports communication between NGHE Solution and external modules including Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as all core update logic is centralized; low diffusion risk.
Limited containment; telemetry data source only; high diffusion if update logic placed here.
Primarily consumer of updated data; poor containment for update logic.
Safety monitoring only; no update logic containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update logic in NGHE Solution minimizes diffusion and supports tight latency control.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution is overloaded.
Single point of failure risk for worksite model updates.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Implement redundancy and load balancing within NGHE Solution to mitigate bottleneck and failure risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well matched by the selected DPs with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk due to centralized containment in NGHE Solution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores strongly favor NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via latency measurements and interface testing on NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of update functionality to NGHE Solution with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add fallback and redundancy mechanisms in NGHE Solution to address containment risks.
Monitor network link performance as it impacts update latency.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy the latency requirement with clear architectural boundaries and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within 0.5 s latency.
FR2: Update the centralized worksite model with processed data ensuring consistency and accuracy within 0.3 s latency.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model data to all relevant modules and stakeholders within 0.2 s latency.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network link latency remains stable and within bounds.
Sufficient processing resources in NGHE Solution.
Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
open_risks: Single point of failure in NGHE Solution.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data under operational load.
System uptime and failure mode analysis.
